3P Learning Ltd. ( (AU:3PL) ) has shared an announcement.

3P Learning Limited has issued a correction to an Appendix 3Y notice previously lodged with the ASX regarding director Matthew Sandblom. The company clarified that the director-related share transactions occurred on 28 April 2026, rather than 18 April 2026 as initially reported.

The rectification underscores 3P Learning’s obligation to maintain accurate disclosure of director interests under ASX listing rules and corporate law. While the change is limited to the transaction date, such corrections are closely watched by investors as part of governance and transparency practices.

data.title: CRISIL Reaffirms Swelect Energy Systems’ Ratings on Bank Loans and NCDs
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Swelect Energy Systems Limited ( (IN:SWELECTES) ) has issued an announcement.

Swelect Energy Systems Limited said CRISIL Ratings has reaffirmed its long-term rating at CRISIL A-/Stable and short-term rating at CRISIL A2+ on bank loan facilities totaling Rs.345 crore. The agency also reaffirmed a CRISIL A (CE)/Stable rating on non-convertible debentures of Rs.138.5 crore, underscoring stable creditworthiness that supports the company’s funding flexibility and stakeholder confidence.

The reaffirmed ratings suggest lenders and investors can view the company’s existing debt profile as relatively secure, with no immediate deterioration in risk perception. This stability in external credit assessment may help Swelect maintain access to bank finance and capital markets on reasonable terms, which is important for sustaining its capital-intensive renewable energy operations and future project pipeline.

More about Swelect Energy Systems Limited

Swelect Energy Systems Limited operates in the renewable energy sector, focusing on solar power solutions and related energy infrastructure. The company engages in projects that typically require substantial bank borrowings and debenture issuances, positioning it as an established player reliant on credit markets to fund its growth and operations.

Average Trading Volume: 1,458

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: 10.14B INR

data.title: Mastermyne Fined $7.3m Over Crinum Mine Industrial Manslaughter, Lodges Appeal
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Metarock Group Limited ( (AU:MYE) ) is now available.

Mastermyne’s wholly owned subsidiary, Mastermyne Crinum Operations Pty Ltd, has been sentenced by the District Court of Queensland to pay a $7.0 million financial penalty plus $0.3 million in regulator’s costs following an industrial manslaughter conviction stemming from the fatal 2021 incident at the Crinum mine. The subsidiary has lodged a Notice of Appeal, signaling that the legal and financial consequences of the case remain unresolved, with potential implications for Mastermyne’s risk profile, governance scrutiny and stakeholder confidence as the proceedings continue.

More about Metarock Group Limited

Mastermyne Group Limited, listed on the ASX, operates in the mining services sector, providing contracted operations and related services to coal mines such as the Crinum mine in Queensland. Through subsidiaries including Mastermyne Crinum Operations Pty Ltd, the company plays a key role in the underground coal mining supply chain and operations management.

Average Trading Volume: 151,110

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$55.56M

data.title: Inaba Denki Sangyo Ends Share Buyback Program Below Authorized Limit
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Inaba Denkisangyo Co., Ltd. ( (JP:9934) ).

Inaba Denki Sangyo Co., Ltd. announced that it has completed a share buyback program authorized by its board on October 31, 2025, but acquired no shares during the most recent period from April 1 to April 30, 2026, because the market price exceeded the company’s initial projections. Despite the unused capacity in the final month, the company had already repurchased a cumulative 1,541,300 shares for ¥3.66 billion under the overall program, which included an adjusted maximum of 2.1 million shares following a two-for-one share split effective December 1, 2025.

The buyback, conducted via market purchases under discretionary trading contracts, fell short of the maximum authorized volume of shares and funds, indicating disciplined capital allocation in response to rising share prices. For shareholders and market participants, the outcome signals that management remains committed to return-of-capital measures while being sensitive to valuation, which may support perceptions of prudent financial policy and potentially influence future capital management decisions.

More about Inaba Denkisangyo Co., Ltd.

Inaba Denki Sangyo Co., Ltd. is a Japan-based trading company listed on the TSE Prime that operates in the electrical equipment and related materials sector. The company focuses on the distribution and sale of electrical components and systems, serving industrial and infrastructure customers in the domestic market and potentially overseas markets as well.

Average Trading Volume: 199,444

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.title: Bourns’ Takeover of Rakon Turns Unconditional as 90% Threshold Passed
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Rakon ( (NZ:RAK) ) just unveiled an update.

Bourns, Inc. has secured acceptances for more than 90% of Rakon’s shares under its $1.55-per-security full takeover bid, satisfying the minimum acceptance condition and allowing the offer to be declared unconditional. Holders who accepted by 1 May will be paid by 8 May, while later acceptances will be settled within five working days, and the offer period has been automatically extended to 15 May under the Takeovers Code.

Having crossed the 90% threshold, Bourns can now move to compulsorily acquire the remaining Rakon equity at the same $1.55 price and subsequently seek Rakon’s delisting from the NZX, effectively ending its status as a listed company. Independent directors continue to urge remaining shareholders and holders of share rights to accept the offer promptly, noting they will receive the same consideration sooner than if they wait for compulsory acquisition, a key timing consideration for investors.

More about Rakon

Rakon Limited is a New Zealand-based technology company that designs and manufactures frequency control products and timing solutions. Its devices are used in applications such as telecommunications infrastructure, positioning and navigation systems, and other high-performance electronics, with shares currently listed on the NZX main board.

Average Trading Volume: 105,986

Current Market Cap: N$351.6M

Learn more about RAK stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Rokeby Resources Calls Physical-Only General Meeting, Urges Early Proxy Voting
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Inca Minerals Limited ( (AU:RKB) ).

Rokeby Resources has called a General Meeting of shareholders for 2 June 2026 at its West Perth offices, with eligibility to vote determined for shareholders on the register as at 5:00pm AWST on 31 May 2026. In line with Corporations Act provisions, the company will not mail hard copies of the Notice of Meeting except to those who have opted in, instead directing investors to access the materials online or via the ASX platform.

The meeting will be conducted solely as a physical gathering, with no virtual participation option, and shareholders are strongly encouraged to lodge proxy votes in advance, which must be received by 11:00am WST on 31 May 2026 to be valid. The notice emphasizes the importance of the resolutions to shareholders’ interests and signals that any change in arrangements will be promptly communicated through ASX announcements and the company’s website, underscoring a continued focus on regulatory compliance and shareholder engagement.

More about Inca Minerals Limited

Rokeby Resources Limited (ASX: RKB) is an Australian-listed resources company based in West Perth, Western Australia. The company is focused on mineral exploration and development, operating within the broader mining and resources sector and targeting investors in the Australian equities market.

Current Market Cap: A$9.13M

data.title: Okamoto Industries Reports No Share Buybacks in April, Majority of Authorization Still Untapped
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Okamoto Industries, Inc. ( (JP:5122) ) has shared an announcement.

Okamoto Industries reported that it did not repurchase any of its common shares between April 1 and April 30, 2026, under its ongoing share buyback program authorized by the board in November 2025. The company noted it treats the period from the end of a quarter to the announcement of financial results as a silent period and therefore refrains from market purchases of its own stock during that time, which contributed to the lack of buybacks in April.

Under the existing authorization, Okamoto is permitted to repurchase up to 190,000 shares, or about 1.11% of outstanding shares, for a maximum of ¥1 billion between November 10, 2025, and September 30, 2026. As of April 30, 2026, it has acquired only 3,200 shares for about ¥17.6 million in total, indicating that most of the approved capacity remains unused and leaving the company flexibility to conduct further buybacks later in the program period depending on market conditions and capital allocation priorities.

More about Okamoto Industries, Inc.

Okamoto Industries, Inc. is a Japanese manufacturer listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Prime Market under securities code 5122. The company is known for its industrial and consumer products, and its shares are actively traded, making capital market actions such as share repurchases an important element of its financial strategy and shareholder return policy.

Average Trading Volume: 18,184

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen98.21B
